summary: Large mnruleplus decompilation PR for the Rules Plus menu. It replaced several placeholder/commented-out functions with C implementations, added menu input handling, option visibility checks, animation update logic, description text management, and GObj/JObj setup for the menu. The decomp.dev bot reported 3 new full matches totaling 2132 bytes and 7 additional improvements, including mn_80233218 reaching 76.29% and several other functions nearing or exceeding 90%. Evidence is mostly from the diff and bot report; there was no human review discussion beyond the tracking note.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; added matched and partially matched Rules Plus menu functions, helper routines, animation tables, extern corrections, and includes.
Header/data-layout file; added RulePlusOption enum, reworked MenuRulesPlusData rule_values into a struct/array union, and refined mn_803ED1D0_t animation fields.

what_changed: Added concrete implementation of fn_8023201C, handling A/Start/B/D-pad input for the Rules Plus menu, saving selected rules, moving between visible options with wraparound, entering stage select, and returning/canceling through existing menu helpers.
Added helper mnRulePlus_SaveRules to copy MenuRulesPlusData rule_values into gmMainLib_8015CC34 global GameRules fields: stock_time_limit, friendly_fire, pause, score_display, and unk_xc/sd_penalty.

Added helper mnRulePlus_IsOptionVisible to centralize game-mode-dependent visibility rules: option 1 hidden for gm_801A4310() == 0x1B, option 3 gated by gmMainLib_8015EE0C(), and option 5 gated by gmMainLib_8015EE44().
Implemented mn_802324E4 for time-limit digit display, using packed JObjIndices to hide/show digit JOBJs and request digit animations for tens/ones.
Implemented mn_80232660 and an inline helper to loop selected animation ranges based on current JObj frame and AnimLoopSettings.
Implemented substantial logic for mn_802327A4, including building option root arrays, updating cursor/selection animations, applying value animations, and refreshing visible options.
Implemented mn_80232D4C for Rules Plus description text creation/removal using HSD_SisLib calls and mn_803ED2E8 description indices.

Implemented fn_80232F44 as the per-frame/process update that watches menu/state/selection/value changes, drives transition animations, updates visuals/text, and writes changed rule values back to GameRules.

Partially implemented mn_80233218, including GObj creation, root JObj loading, user_data allocation, rule initialization from GameRules, option/cursor/value JObj creation, and initial description text setup.

Reworked static animation/data tables: mn_803ED1D0 now uses grouped x64[2] and x7C[2] animation ranges, mn_803ED270 and mn_803ED294 were populated with real frame ranges, and JObjIndices replaced raw packed s32 constants for mn_804DBE40/mn_804DBE48.
Updated mnruleplus.h by adding enum RulePlusOption and exposing rule_values both as named fields and a u8 values array for indexed option handling.

Corrected/expanded declarations and dependencies, including MenuKindData mn_803EB6B0[] instead of pointer, extern f32 mn_804D6BE4, and includes for debug, gobjplink, sislib, gm_1A3F, lb_00F9, lbaudio_ax, mnmainrule, mnstagesw, and dolphin/os.
smart_moves: Introduced mnRulePlus_SaveRules to remove repeated GameRules copy code; the same mapping is reused in confirm/cancel/process paths.
Centralized option visibility logic in mnRulePlus_IsOptionVisible, making the D-pad wrap loops easier to match and making mode-gated options explicit.
Used a union for MenuRulesPlusData.rule_values so code can use descriptive field names for GameRules mapping while also indexing values by hovered_selection for generic menu logic.
Used a JObjIndices union with both packed s32 and u8 idx[4] views, preserving packed data layout while allowing natural per-digit loops over JObj indices.
Refactored mn_803ED1D0_t animation fields from separate x64/x70/x7C/x88 members into arrays for unselected/selected variants, matching repeated access patterns in cursor animation code.
Preserved matching-oriented local scaffolding such as fixed-size jobj_map[17], jobj_parts[17], explicit stack padding via PAD_STACK, and careful casts to u8/u16/s32 around selection values.
Added HSD_ASSERTREPORT around user_data allocation in mn_80233218, replacing the prior decompiler-style OSReport/assert placeholder with project-idiomatic assert code.
decomp_lessons: When menu options share storage and behavior, a named-field plus array union can improve both readability and matching: named fields for semantic writes, values[index] for generic selection update paths.
Packed constants that are iterated byte-by-byte are good candidates for a union layout; this avoids magic pointer arithmetic while preserving exact byte ordering and static data shape.
For menu decompilation, factor repeated visibility predicates early. The same predicate may appear in input navigation, option construction, and visual refresh logic.
Animation tables often become clearer and closer to source when logically paired frame ranges are represented as small arrays, such as selected/unselected animation pairs.
Keep explicit integer-width casts around menu selection and rule values. The diff frequently casts hovered_selection and confirmed_selection to u8/u16/s32, indicating width sensitivity for matching.
Large placeholder replacements can be staged: this PR fully matched smaller functions while leaving large object-construction code only partially matched, still producing measurable progress.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for prioritizing future work: fn_8023201C, fn_80232F44, and mn_80232660 reached 100%, while mn_80233218 and mn_802327A4 remained follow-up targets.
naming_conventions: New semantic helper names use the module prefix and Pascal-style suffix: mnRulePlus_SaveRules and mnRulePlus_IsOptionVisible.

RulePlusOption enum entries use RULEPLUS_* uppercase constants: RULEPLUS_TIME_LIMIT, RULEPLUS_FRIENDLY_FIRE, RULEPLUS_PAUSE, RULEPLUS_SCORE, RULEPLUS_SD_PENALTY, RULEPLUS_STAGE_SELECT, RULEPLUS_COUNT.
Data-layout fields in unknown structs remain offset-like where semantics are not fully known, such as x0, xE, x4C, x58, x64, x7C, x94, xC, and x34.
The PR keeps existing address-based function names where no better symbol is evidenced: fn_8023201C, mn_802324E4, mn_80232660, mn_802327A4, mn_80232D4C, fn_80232F44, mn_80233218.
JObjIndices is a descriptive local typedef for a packed JObj index value, with fields packed and idx.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in newly implemented functions, including PAD_STACK(0x30), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(0x18), to control stack layout for matching.
Kept local arrays with exact sizes and initialization patterns, such as u16 jobj_map[17] and HSD_JObj* jobj_parts[17], mirroring decompiled control flow.
Maintained explicit button bit checks for input handling: 0x200 for A, 0x100 for Start, 0x20 for B, 1/2 for D-pad up/down, and 4/8 for left/right.
Preserved width-sensitive casts around menu state, hovered_selection, confirmed_selection, and rule values to influence generated code shape.
Converted raw decompiler comments/placeholders into C while retaining some matching-friendly structure, including explicit if/else visibility blocks rather than overly compressed boolean expressions.
Used animation helper mn_8022ED6C and direct HSD_JObjReqAnim/HSD_JObjAnim calls according to whether the code needed loop settings or one-shot frame requests.
Represented selected/unselected animation frame groups as arrays so indexed expressions can match repeated pointer arithmetic in the original.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased by 0.05% (+2132 bytes) for GALE01 162f46a - 61f0e8a.
Reported 3 new full matches: fn_8023201C (+1084 bytes), fn_80232F44 (+724 bytes), and mn_80232660 (+324 bytes), all in main/melee/mn/mnruleplus.
Reported 7 improvements in unmatched items: mn_80233218 to 76.29%, mn_802327A4 to 65.88%, mn_80232D4C to 93.10%, mn_802324E4 to 96.37%, .data to 94.25%, .sdata2 to 80.77%, and .sbss to 66.67%.
No substantive human review comments were present in the provided slice; only the PR tracking note was included.

follow_up_queries: Investigate remaining mismatches in mn_80233218, which the bot reported at 76.29% after this PR.
Investigate remaining mismatches in mn_802327A4, which the bot reported at 65.88% after this PR.
Check whether mn_802324E4 at 96.37% and mn_80232D4C at 93.10% need small type, cast, or control-flow adjustments to fully match.
Look for later PRs that rename address-based mnruleplus functions or replace offset-like fields xC/x34/x64/x7C with semantic names.
Compare visibility helper mnRulePlus_IsOptionVisible with other menu modules to see if the option gating logic can be shared or renamed.
